subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(1-5)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions:
Eight persons– A, B, C, D, P, Q, R and S (not necessarily in the same order) sit in two rows i.e. Row 1 and Row 2, both the rows are parallel to each other. Row 1 is to the north of Row 2. Four persons sit in each row. A, B, C and D sit in Row 1 and face south while P, Q, R and S sit in Row 2 and face north.
R sits opposite to C. One person sit between A and the one who faces S. C doesn’t sit at any of the ends. S does not sit at any of the ends. The one who faces P sits two persons away from D. Q does not sit left of P.

Q1. Who among the following person sits opposite to one who sits two persons away from B?
q:
Directions (1-5): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions:
Eight persons– A, B, C, D, P, Q, R and S (not necessarily in the same order) sit in two rows i.e. Row 1 and Row 2, both the rows are parallel to each other. Row 1 is to the north of Row 2. Four persons sit in each row. A, B, C and D sit in Row 1 and face south while P, Q, R and S sit in Row 2 and face north.
R sits opposite to C. One person sit between A and the one who faces S. C doesn’t sit at any of the ends. S does not sit at any of the ends. The one who faces P sits two persons away from D. Q does not sit left of P.

Q1. Who among the following person sits opposite to one who sits two persons away from B?
explanation: Row 1 (facing south): A, C, D, B. Row 2 (facing north): P, R, S, Q. B sits at the right end (pos 4). The person sitting two persons away from B is C (pos 2). The person opposite C is R.

Q2. Which of the following statements is not true?

options.text: A sits at one of the ends.
R sits immediately left of S.
C sits two persons away from B.

explanation: Q sits at the end and faces B. D sits at pos 3 and faces S. Thus, 'Q faces D' is not true.

options.text: As many persons sit to the left of P

explanation: Since Row 1 faces south, left is to the east. D sits at pos 3, so only 1 person (B) sits to his left.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(6-8)$: In each of the questions, relationships between some elements are shown in the statements. These statements are followed by conclusions numbered I and II. Read the statements and give the answer.

Q6. $Statements: A < B ≤ C = D, D > E ≥ F > G$
Conclusions:
I. D ≥ B
II. G < C
q:
Directions (6-8): In each of the questions, relationships between some elements are shown in the statements. These statements are followed by conclusions numbered I and II. Read the statements and give the answer.

Q6. $Statements: A < B ≤ C = D, D > E ≥ F > G$
Conclusions:
I. D ≥ B
II. G < C

options.text: If only conclusion I is true.
If only conclusion II is true.
If either conclusion I or II is true.
If both conclusions I and II are true.
If neither conclusion I nor II is true.

explanation: $I: B ≤ C = D implies D ≥ B (True). II: C = D > E ≥ F > G implies C > G (True). Both are true$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(6-8)$: In each of the questions, relationships between some elements are shown in the statements. These statements are followed by conclusions numbered I and II. Read the statements and give the answer.

Q7. $Statements: P < Q > R ≤ S > T ≤ U = V$
Conclusions:
I. P > V
II. U ≥ P
q:
Directions (6-8): In each of the questions, relationships between some elements are shown in the statements. These statements are followed by conclusions numbered I and II. Read the statements and give the answer.

Q7. $Statements: P < Q > R ≤ S > T ≤ U = V$
Conclusions:
I. P > V
II. U ≥ P

options.text: If only conclusion I is true.
If only conclusion II is true.
If either conclusion I or II is true.
If both conclusions I and II are true.
If neither conclusion I nor II is true.

explanation: There are opposite symbols between P and V, and P and U. No direct comparison is possible. Neither follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(6-8)$: In each of the questions, relationships between some elements are shown in the statements. These statements are followed by conclusions numbered I and II. Read the statements and give the answer.

Q8. $Statements: X = Y < Z ≤ W, Y > V ≥ U ≤ A < B$
Conclusions:
I. Z > B
II. X ≤ V
q:
Directions (6-8): In each of the questions, relationships between some elements are shown in the statements. These statements are followed by conclusions numbered I and II. Read the statements and give the answer.

Q8. $Statements: X = Y < Z ≤ W, Y > V ≥ U ≤ A < B$
Conclusions:
I. Z > B
II. X ≤ V

options.text: If only conclusion I is true.
If only conclusion II is true.
If either conclusion I or II is true.
If both conclusions I and II are true.
If neither conclusion I nor II is true.

explanation: $I: The relationship between Z and B cannot be established. II: X = Y > V implies X > V, so X ≤ V is false. Neither follows$.

G & 7 8 H * F 5 B U W % A 4 S 3 L K O P 1 Q 2 E N

Q9. Which element is third to the left of eighth element from right end?
q:
Directions (9-13): Study the following alphanumericsymbol series carefully and answer the questions given below:
G & 7 8 H * F 5 B U W % A 4 S 3 L K O P 1 Q 2 E N

Q9. Which element is third to the left of eighth element from right end?
explanation: The 8th element from the right end is K. The 3rd element to the left of K is S.

q:
Directions (9-13): Study the following alphanumericsymbol series carefully and answer the questions given below:
G & 7 8 H * F 5 B U W % A 4 S 3 L K O P 1 Q 2 E N

Q10. How many numbers are there which are immediately preceded and immediately followed by a letter?
explanation: The numbers preceded and followed by letters are: 4 (in A4S), 3 (in S3L), 1 (in P1Q), and 2 (in Q2E). There are four such numbers.

Q11. What is the sum of all the prime numbers?
q:
Directions (9-13): Study the following alphanumericsymbol series carefully and answer the questions given below:
G & 7 8 H * F 5 B U W % A 4 S 3 L K O P 1 Q 2 E N

Q11. What is the sum of all the prime numbers?
explanation: $The prime numbers in the series are 7, 5, 3, and 2. Sum = 7 + 5 + 3 + 2 = 17$.

q:
Directions (9-13): Study the following alphanumericsymbol series carefully and answer the questions given below:
G & 7 8 H * F 5 B U W % A 4 S 3 L K O P 1 Q 2 E N

Q12. If we remove all the symbols from the given series, then which element will be ninth from the left end?
explanation: The series without symbols is: G, 7, 8, H, F, 5, B, U, W, A... The 9th element is W, which is not in the options (so None of these).

q:

Q13. How many symbols are there which are immediately preceded by a letter but not immediately followed by a number?
explanation: The symbols preceded by letters and not followed by numbers are: * (in H*F) and % (in W%A). There are two such symbols.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Find odd one out?
q: Find odd one out?
explanation: $The pattern is: first letter (+3) -> second letter (2) -> third letter. BEC (B+3=E, E2=C), DGE, MPN, and ORP follow this. GJK fails (G+3=J, but J2=H, not K)$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(15-19)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions:
Seven persons F, G, H, I, J, K and L are living in a sevenstorey building but not necessarily in the same order. The bottommost floor in numbered as 1 and the top most floor is numbered as 7.
As many floors are there above F as below K. J lives above G but not above F. F lives above third floor but not on fifth floor. Three floors are there between the floor on which J lives and the floor on which H lives. H lives below J. L lives just above G but on a prime numbered floor.

Q15. Which of the following statements is correct?
q:
Directions (15-19): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions:
Seven persons F, G, H, I, J, K and L are living in a sevenstorey building but not necessarily in the same order. The bottommost floor in numbered as 1 and the top most floor is numbered as 7.
As many floors are there above F as below K. J lives above G but not above F. F lives above third floor but not on fifth floor. Three floors are there between the floor on which J lives and the floor on which H lives. H lives below J. L lives just above G but on a prime numbered floor.

Q15. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: L lives on the topmost floor.
H lives on the third floor.
F lives on the sixth floor.
G lives on the fourth floor.

explanation: Floor layout (7 to 1): Floor 7 - F, Floor 6 - J, Floor 5 - L, Floor 4 - G, Floor 3 - I, Floor 2 - H, Floor 1 - K. Hence, 'G lives on the fourth floor' is correct.

Q19. Four of the following five are in the same group, who among the following does not belong to that group?
explanation: K (Floor 1), I (Floor 3), L (Floor 5), and F (Floor 7) all live on odd floors. J (Floor 6) lives on an even floor.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: If we form a fourletter meaningful word by using the first, second, third and ninth letters from the left end of the word 'SELECTION', then which of the following will be the third letter from left end of the meaningful word thus formed. If more than one such meaningful word is formed mark X as your answer. If no meaningful word is formed, mark Z as your answer?
q: If we form a fourletter meaningful word by using the first, second, third and ninth letters from the left end of the word 'SELECTION', then which of the following will be the third letter from left end of the meaningful word thus formed. If more than one such meaningful word is formed mark X as your answer. If no meaningful word is formed, mark Z as your answer?
explanation: The chosen letters from SELECTION are S, E, L, and N. The only meaningful word is 'LENS'. Its third letter from left end is N.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(21-23)$: In each question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions definitely follows from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Choose the appropriate answer:

Q21. Statements:
Some tops are not Jeans.
No Hats are Shirts.
Only a few tops are Shirts.
Conclusions:
I. $Some Hats are not Tops$
II. All Jeans are Shirts is a possibility.
q:
Directions (21-23): In each question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions definitely follows from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Choose the appropriate answer:

Q21. Statements:
Some tops are not Jeans.
No Hats are Shirts.
Only a few tops are Shirts.
Conclusions:
I. $Some Hats are not Tops$
II. All Jeans are Shirts is a possibility.

options.text: Either conclusion I or II follows
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow

explanation: I is false (no negative link between Hats and Tops). II is true (no restriction between Jeans and Shirts). Only II follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(21-23)$: In each question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions definitely follows from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Choose the appropriate answer:

Q22. Statements:
All Boats are Ships.
Only a few Ships are Submarine.
A few Submarine are Motorboat.
Conclusions:
I. All Ships can be Submarine.
II. Some Boats are Motorboat.
q:
Directions (21-23): In each question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Conclusions:
I. All Ships can be Submarine.
II. Some Boats are Motorboat.

options.text: Either conclusion I or II follows
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow

explanation: I is false ('Only a few Ships are Submarine' means all Ships cannot be Submarine). II is false (no direct relationship between Boats and Motorboat). Neither follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(24-28)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Six persons E, F, G, H, I and J purchase books one after the another, but not necessarily in the same order. Also, they are ranked 1 to 6 from starting to last purchase respectively.
F purchases book four persons before J. Two persons purchase books between J and H. I purchases book before G. E purchases book before J but after I. I doesn’t purchase book immediately before E. G doesn’t purchase book immediately before J.

Q24. How many persons purchase books before E?
q:
Directions (24-28): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Six persons E, F, G, H, I and J purchase books one after the another, but not necessarily in the same order. Also, they are ranked 1 to 6 from starting to last purchase respectively.
F purchases book four persons before J. Two persons purchase books between J and H. I purchases book before G. E purchases book before J but after I. I doesn’t purchase book immediately before E. G doesn’t purchase book immediately before J.

Q24. How many persons purchase books before E?
explanation: Purchase Rank order: 1 - I, 2 - F, 3 - H, 4 - G, 5 - E, 6 - J. There are 4 persons before E.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(24-28)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Six persons E, F, G, H, I and J purchase books one after the another, but not necessarily in the same order. Also, they are ranked 1 to 6 from starting to last purchase respectively.
F purchases book four persons before J. Two persons purchase books between J and H. I purchases book before G. E purchases book before J but after I. I doesn’t purchase book immediately before E. G doesn’t purchase book immediately before J.

Q26. If all the persons are arranged in alphabetical order in the sequence from first rank to last rank, then position of how many persons remain unchanged?
q:
Directions (24-28): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Six persons E, F, G, H, I and J purchase books one after the another, but not necessarily in the same order. Also, they are ranked 1 to 6 from starting to last purchase respectively.
F purchases book four persons before J. Two persons purchase books between J and H. I purchases book before G. E purchases book before J but after I. I doesn’t purchase book immediately before E. G doesn’t purchase book immediately before J.

Q26. If all the persons are arranged in alphabetical order in the sequence from first rank to last rank, then position of how many persons remain unchanged?
explanation: Original: I, F, H, G, E, J. Alphabetical: E, F, G, H, I, J. The positions of F (Rank 2) and J (Rank 6) remain unchanged (two persons).

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(24-28)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Six persons E, F, G, H, I and J purchase books one after the another, but not necessarily in the same order. Also, they are ranked 1 to 6 from starting to last purchase respectively.
F purchases book four persons before J. Two persons purchase books between J and H. I purchases book before G. E purchases book before J but after I. I doesn’t purchase book immediately before E. G doesn’t purchase book immediately before J.

Q27. Which among the following statements is/are true?
I. F purchases the book before I.
II. More than two persons purchase books between I and J.
III. E purchases the book after G.
q:
Directions (24-28): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Six persons E, F, G, H, I and J purchase books one after the another, but not necessarily in the same order. Also, they are ranked 1 to 6 from starting to last purchase respectively.
F purchases book four persons before J. Two persons purchase books between J and H. I purchases book before G. E purchases book before J but after I. I doesn’t purchase book immediately before E. G doesn’t purchase book immediately before J.

Q27. Which among the following statements is/are true?
I. F purchases the book before I.
II. More than two persons purchase books between I and J.
III. E purchases the book after G.
explanation: I is false. II is true (4 persons between I and J). III is true (E at 5, G at 4). Thus, Both II and III are true.

Q30. What is the difference between the highest digit of the second lowest number and the lowest digit of the third highest number in the given number series?
explanation: $Numbers sorted: 218,356,427,534,878. Second lowest is 356, its highest digit is 6. Third highest is 427, its lowest digit is 2. Difference = 6-2 = 4$.

q: In the word “COMPLEX”, how many pairs of letters have the same number of letters between them (both forward and backward direction) in the word as in the alphabet?
explanation: The only pair is O and L (O is at pos 2, L is at pos 5, gap is 2 letters; in alphabet, L and O have 2 letters M, N in between).

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(38-40)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Viraj started walking from Point X in the east direction for 10m and reached Point Y. From Point Y, he turned right and walked for 15m and reached Point Z. From Point Z, he turned left and walked for 7m and reached Point W. Point S is 5m west Point T. From Point W, he turned left and walked for 4m and reached Point V. From Point V, he turned right and walked for 5m and reached Point U. Point U is 11m south of Point T.

Q38. If Point W is 7m to the west of Point B then in which direction is Point T with respect to Point B?
q:
Directions (38-40): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Viraj started walking from Point X in the east direction for 10m and reached Point Y. From Point Y, he turned right and walked for 15m and reached Point Z. From Point Z, he turned left and walked for 7m and reached Point W. Point S is 5m west Point T. From Point W, he turned left and walked for 4m and reached Point V. From Point V, he turned right and walked for 5m and reached Point U. Point U is 11m south of Point T.

Q38. If Point W is 7m to the west of Point B then in which direction is Point T with respect to Point B?
explanation: $Coordinates: W=(17, 15), B=(24, 15), T=(22, 0). T is situated to the NorthWest \text{ of } B$.

Q40. What is the total distance travelled by Viraj from Point Z to Point T?
q:
Directions (38-40): Study the following information carefully and answer the given questions below:
Viraj started walking from Point X in the east direction for 10m and reached Point Y. From Point Y, he turned right and walked for 15m and reached Point Z. From Point Z, he turned left and walked for 7m and reached Point W. Point S is 5m west Point T. From Point W, he turned left and walked for 4m and reached Point V. From Point V, he turned right and walked for 5m and reached Point U. Point U is 11m south of Point T.

Q40. What is the total distance travelled by Viraj from Point Z to Point T?
explanation: $Path: Z -> W (7m) -> V (4m) -> U (5m) -> T (11m). Total distance = 7 + 4 + 5 + 11 = 27m$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The average marks obtained by a student in five subjects is 105. If we exclude marks of three subjects which average is 72 excluding highest and lowest mark obtained by the student, then find the sum of highest and lowest mark obtained by the student?
q: The average marks obtained by a student in five subjects is 105. If we exclude marks of three subjects which average is 72 excluding highest and lowest mark obtained by the student, then find the sum of highest and lowest mark obtained by the student?
explanation: $Total marks = 105 * 5 = 525. Excluded subjects sum = 72 * 3 = 216. Sum \text{ of } highest + lowest = 525-216 = 309$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: 6 bulls can plug a field in 9 days, while 12 ox’s can plug the field in 6 days. Find in how many days 5 bulls and 4 ox’s can plug the same field?
q: 6 bulls can plug a field in 9 days, while 12 ox’s can plug the field in 6 days. Find in how many days 5 bulls and 4 ox’s can plug the same field?
explanation: $6B * 9 = 12O * 6 ⇒ 3B = 4O ⇒ B:O = 4:3. Total work = 216 units. Efficiency \text{ of } (5B + 4O) = 20 + 12 = 32. Days = 216/32 = 6.75 days (closest option is 6)$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The length of a rectangle is 3 cm more than its breadth. If the perimeter of the rectangle is 66 cm, then find the area of the rectangle (in cm square)?
q: The length of a rectangle is 3 cm more than its breadth. If the perimeter of the rectangle is 66 cm, then find the area of the rectangle (in cm square)?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A vessel contains mixture of milk and water in the ratio of $11:8$. When 8 liters of milk and 24 liters of water added in the mixture, then the ratio of milk to water in resultant mixture becomes $7:6$. Find the quantity of water in the initial mixture?
q: A vessel contains mixture of milk and water in the ratio of 11:8. When 8 liters of milk and 24 liters of water added in the mixture, then the ratio of milk to water in resultant mixture becomes 7:6. Find the quantity of water in the initial mixture?
explanation: $Initial: milk = 11x, water = 8x. (11x+8)/(8x+24) = 7/6 ⇒ 66x + 48 = 56x + 168 ⇒ 10x = 120 ⇒ x = 12. Initial water = 8x = 96 liters$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: There are two numbers and smaller number is 13 more than half off the bigger number. If difference between bigger and smaller number is 78, then find the smaller number?
q: There are two numbers and smaller number is 13 more than half off the bigger number. If difference between bigger and smaller number is 78, then find the smaller number?
explanation: $Let bigger = B, smaller = S. S = B/2 + 13 ⇒ 2S = B + 26. Also B - S = 78 ⇒ B = S + 78. Substituting B: 2S = S + 104 ⇒ S = 104$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: X is 24 more than Y and $\frac{3}{5}$th of X is 16 less than Y. Find the value of ‘Y’?
q: X is 24 more than Y and 3/5th of X is 16 less than Y. Find the value of ‘Y’?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Three years ago, the age of A was 21 years. Four years hence, the ratio of age of A to that of B will be $7:5$. Find the present age of B (in years)?
q: Three years ago, the age of A was 21 years. Four years hence, the ratio of age of A to that of B will be 7:5. Find the present age of B (in years)?
explanation: $A's present age = 24. Four years hence A = 28. Since A:B = 7:5 at that time, B's age will be 20. B's present age = 16 years$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: P started business with Rs 2500 and after 4 months, Q joined him with Rs. X. At the end of one year, the ratio of profit share of P and Q is $5:4$. Find the value of $X$ (in Rs.)?
q: P started business with Rs 2500 and after 4 months, Q joined him with Rs. X. At the end of one year, the ratio of profit share of P and Q is 5:4. Find the value of X (in Rs.)?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude

q: A man invested Rs. P on simple interest at rate of 12% p.a. for five years and he received Rs 2700 as interest. Find the value of P (in Rs.)?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A man travelled a certain distance by car for 2 hours at 60 km/hr, by Bus for 5 hours at 80 km/hr and the remaining distance by bicycle at 10 km/hr for 5 hours. Find the average speed of man in the whole journey?
q: A man travelled a certain distance by car for 2 hours at 60 km/hr, by Bus for 5 hours at 80 km/hr and the remaining distance by bicycle at 10 km/hr for 5 hours. Find the average speed of man in the whole journey?
explanation: $Total distance = (2*60) + (5*80) + (5*10) = 120 + 400 + 50 = 570 km. Total time = 12 hours. Average speed = 570/12 = 47.5 km/hr$.

question:
Directions $(61-65)$: The table given below shows number of students in three classes A, B and C in four different months March, April, May and June. Read the data carefully and answer the questions given below.

Class Data Table:
Months | Class A | Class B | Class C
--------|---------|---------|---------
March | 96 | 87 | 91
April | 64 | 72 | 60
May | 90 | 80 | 68
June | 75 | 63 | 56
Q61. Find the difference between total number of students in class B & C together in May and total number of students in class A & C together in March?
q:
Directions (61-65): The table given below shows number of students in three classes A, B and C in four different months March, April, May and June. Read the data carefully and answer the questions given below.

Class Data Table:
Months | Class A | Class B | Class C
--------|---------|---------|---------
March | 96 | 87 | 91
April | 64 | 72 | 60
May | 90 | 80 | 68
June | 75 | 63 | 56
Q61. Find the difference between total number of students in class B & C together in May and total number of students in class A & C together in March?
explanation: $B+C in May = 80 + 68 = 148. A+C in March = 96 + 91 = 187. Difference = 187-148 = 39$.

Q63. If total number of students in class D in month of April are half of the total student in class A and B in the same month, then find the sum of total students in class D in month of April and students in class B in month of March?
explanation: $A+B in April = 64 + 72 = 136 ⇒ D in April = 68. B in March = 87. Sum = 68 + 87 = 155$.
